title: Fix math question not displaying during gameplay

requirements.summary: Fix the game screen so that the arithmetic problem is visibly rendered during active gameplay with proper contrast and immediate updates
acceptanceCriteria: When the game starts, an arithmetic problem (e.g., '7 × 8 = ?') is displayed prominently on the game screen.
The problem text is visible against the dark background with sufficient contrast.
A new problem appears immediately after the player submits an answer (correct or incorrect).
The problem display does not remain blank or show undefined/null at any point during an active game session.
The problem is rendered in large, readable typography consistent with the bold visual theme.

file_operations.description: Debug and fix the problem display rendering by ensuring the problem data from useGameState is correctly displayed in the JSX. Verify the question element is not conditionally hidden by loading/undefined checks, add fallback rendering if problem is temporarily null, ensure text color has sufficient contrast against the dark background (use neon or electric theme tokens), and confirm the typography is large and readable. Add defensive null checks and ensure the problem updates immediately when currentProblem changes.

Review and fix the problem data flow from the generateProblem backend call to ensure currentProblem is set immediately when the game starts and updates synchronously after each answer submission. Verify the hook correctly manages the problem state lifecycle, handles the initial fetch on mount, and triggers a new problem fetch after handleSubmitAnswer. Add console logging or error handling if the backend query fails or returns undefined.
